title: Minuartia quadrivalvis
taxonomy: Minuartia quadrivalvis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Minuartia, which includes small to medium-sized annual or perennial plants. The genus Minuartia is a part of the larger family Caryophyllaceae, which comprises about 110 genera and 2,000 species. intro: Minuartia quadrivalvis, also known as Four-valued Minuartia or Four-valued Sandwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and four-angled stem. Traditionally, Minuartia quadrivalvis has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Minuartia quadrivalvis is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in some traditional herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
